source.title: Active interoceptive inference and the emotional brain
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

confidence_rationale: Friston et al. provide formal predictive coding architecture for distinct interoceptive (body/self) vs exteroceptive (world) generative models, with separate prediction error streams — directly supporting the z_self/z_world split in SD-005. Also supports MECH-069 (incommensurable error signals): the paper explicitly treats interoceptive and exteroceptive prediction errors as structurally distinct, not reducible to each other.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The brain maintains distinct generative models for interoceptive (internal body state) and exteroceptive (external world state) signals; these generate separate prediction error streams that are not interchangeable; autonomic reflexes minimize interoceptive prediction error while allostatic behaviour minimizes exteroceptive prediction error; emotion arises from interoceptive inference contextualized by exteroceptive cues.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-005 self/world latent split: z_self (E2 domain, proprioceptive/interoceptive, motor-sensory prediction error) and z_world (E3 domain, exteroceptive, harm/goal prediction error) correspond directly to the interoceptive vs exteroceptive generative model split. MECH-069: the incommensurability of error signals across three BG loops is grounded in the structural distinction between interoceptive and exteroceptive prediction error channels, which cannot be collapsed without misattributing credit.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Friston's active inference framework is a normative model, not a direct biological mechanism report. The specific claim that these two streams are separately implemented in identifiable brain regions (insula for interoception, prefrontal/parietal for exteroception) has empirical support, but the specific latent dimension split proposed in SD-005 (z_self vs z_world as separate encoder heads) is a REE architectural instantiation, not directly stated in the source.
mapping.source_context: Formal free-energy/active-inference framework; human autonomic, interoceptive, and emotional processing literature synthesis.

confidence_components.notes: Normative framework with strong empirical grounding; mapping to SD-005 latent split is structurally faithful; the encoder-architecture specifics are REE implementation choices not directly evidenced.
